weaknesses: No NCII or sensitive content review experience
No explicit thought leadership in T&S
No documented enforcement quality or calibration experience
improvement_bullets: Collaborated cross-functionally with Underwriting and Product teams to perform operational monitoring and error resolution, identifying data discrepancies that improved internal policy compliance and accuracy by 30%.
Implemented automated anomaly detection workflows to identify high-risk patterns in loan data, demonstrating proactive harm-fighting and risk-mitigation strategies through data-driven modeling.
Facilitated stakeholder calibrations and project documentation to align operational workflows with quality standards, ensuring consistent enforcement quality across project lifecycles.
Conducted complex analysis on customer segments and risk profiles, utilizing machine learning-informed segmentation to provide actionable recommendations for strategy optimization.
Managed cross-functional escalations and workflow bottlenecks, streamlining project delivery timelines and providing data-backed solutions to improve team operational efficiency.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Trust & Safety Analyst opportunity with Russell Tobin. I am interested in this opportunity because of Google's commitment to creating a safe online environment and the critical nature of the work performed within the Trust & Safety domain. What interests me about working for Russell Tobin is its focus on high-impact, data-driven solutions; this excites me because it mirrors my own passion for using advanced analytics to solve complex human and operational challenges. I believe my expertise in data-oriented analysis and my strength in cross-functional stakeholder management would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex, sensitive datasets into actionable operational strategies. In fact, while at Haven Technologies, I collaborated with Underwriting and Product teams to reconcile insurance testing data, where I designed unified data schemas and resolved critical workflow bottlenecks. This work improved overall project delivery speed by 25% and directly enhanced the reliability of the tools used by our internal analysts. Ultimately, I know that the Trust & Safety Analyst role at Russell Tobin would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in SQL and Python to the task of architecting metrics for harm-fighting and policy enforcement. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my operational monitoring and process optimization skills. I overhauled reporting workflows by automating intake systems via Zapier and building Power BI dashboards, which reduced processing time by 40% and improved decision-making accuracy. I am excited by the opportunity to use my analytical rigor in working on critical content review and policy enforcement at Russell Tobin. I know that my technical fluency and commitment to operational excellence align with what it takes to succeed at the Trust & Safety Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
